role: Minister of Commerce (2005–2009); Minister of Petroleum (2009–2011); head of Plan and Budget Organization under Raisi; IRGC-affiliated
bio:
Masoud Mir-Kazemi is an Iranian principlist politician and former [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) member who served in two cabinet posts under [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad): [Minister of Commerce](/orgs/ministry-of-commerce) (2005–2009) and [Minister of Petroleum](/orgs/ministry-of-petroleum) (2009–2011).

After the 1979 revolution, Mir-Kazemi joined the revolution committees and then the IRGC. He holds a PhD in industrial engineering from [Tarbiat Modares University](/orgs/tarbiat-modares-university). He served as a member of the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) representing Tehran from 2012 to 2016.

In 2021, President Ebrahim Raisi appointed Mir-Kazemi as vice president and head of the Plan and Budget Organization, continuing his role in Iran's economic management.

sources.title: Raisi appoints Masoud Mir-Kazemi as new PBO head

editorial_notes: Born 1960. IRGC member post-revolution. PhD industrial engineering Tarbiat Modares. MP Tehran 2012-2016. VP and PBO head under Raisi 2021.
